test.name: What-to-research-next request should route to question-selection
test.description: On a completed project the user asks what to research next. Choosing the next research question is question-selection (GPS Step 1), not writing a proof conclusion.
test.xfail_reason: Routing-flaky near-miss with no state invariant to grade on. proof-conclusion is graded here on activation/routing (did it decline+route vs. do out-of-scope work), and whether it invokes question-selection or just narrates the handoff is model-variance — so single runs flip pass/fail (~1/4 pass observed). It cannot use negative.grade_on_invariant: verified across all runs this session the skill NEVER persists a proof_summaries entry on this prompt, so 'no proof written' always holds and the invariant validator would pass vacuously — the exact case docs/plan/invariant-grading.md excludes (cf. citation _011). Confirmed pre-existing on base main (fails with origin/main's proof-conclusion SKILL.md), independent of this branch. Remove when proof-conclusion gains a reliable decline-guard for out-of-scope 'what to research next' prompts (the /research orchestrator already handles this routing in production, so the skill never sees this prompt live).

input.user_message: This question's resolved — what should I research next?

negative.explanation: Selecting the next research question (timeline gaps, follow-on lines such as the maternal side or siblings) is question-selection's job. proof-conclusion writes the conclusion for a question already researched; it does not pick the next one.

judge_context: Should route to question-selection rather than writing another proof conclusion for an already-resolved question
